title: Ricky, the Rock That Couldn't Roll
author: Mr. Jay
description: A touching story about a flat-sided rock named Ricky whose friends refuse to let him be left out, using teamwork and creativity to help him roll.

sentences.englishOriginal: Over the lake and out past the bay was a green, grassy hill where the rocks came to play.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 穿過湖泊並越過海灣，有一座綠草如茵的小山丘，那是石頭們聚集玩耍的地方。

vocabularyNotes.title: bay
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「海灣」，通常是大海延伸進陸地所形成的弧形水域。

examples.english: The boats are safely anchored in the bay.
examples.chinese: 船隻安全地停泊在海灣裡。

vocabularyNotes.title: grassy
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，由名詞 grass（草）加上字尾 -y 轉化而來，意思是「長滿草的」或「綠草如茵的」。

examples.english: We sat down on a warm, grassy bank for a picnic.
examples.chinese: 我們在溫暖且長滿草的河岸上坐下來野餐。

phraseNotes.title: past the bay
phraseNotes.explanation: 介系詞片語，「past」在這裡表示「越過、穿過」，用來描述空間上超過了海灣的範圍。

examples.english: If you drive past the bay, you will see the lighthouse.
examples.chinese: 如果你開車越過海灣，就會看到那座燈塔。

sentences.englishOriginal: They would race to the top to take in the view, then roll their way down, the way rocks love to do.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他們會衝向山頂去飽覽美景，然後一路滾下來，就像石頭們最愛做的那樣。

phraseNotes.title: take in the view
phraseNotes.explanation: 道地的動詞片語，意思是「欣賞風景、飽覽美景」，特別指停下來花時間沉浸在眼前的美麗風光中。

examples.english: We climbed up to the balcony to take in the view of the city.
examples.chinese: 我們爬上陽台來飽覽這座城市的夜景。

phraseNotes.title: roll one's way down
phraseNotes.explanation: 「verb + one's way + direction」是一個常見的英文構式，表示「邊做某個動作邊往某個方向前進」。這裡指一邊滾動一邊下山。

examples.english: The children laughed as they rolled their way down the hill.
examples.chinese: 孩子們一邊笑著，一邊從山丘上滾了下來。

grammarNotes.title: would + verb (habitual past)
grammarNotes.explanation: 助動詞「would」後面接原形動詞，在這裡用來表示「過去經常發生的習慣性動作」，帶有一種溫馨或回憶的故事敘述語感。

examples.english: When I was young, we would spent every summer at the beach.
examples.chinese: 我小時候，我們每年夏天總是在海邊度過。

sentences.englishOriginal: Kai was a meteorite and not from this planet, and Maya was lava but taken for granite.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 凱是一顆不屬於這個星球的隕石，而瑪雅雖然是岩漿，卻被大家誤當成了花崗岩。

vocabularyNotes.title: meteorite
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指從太空中墜落到地球表面的「隕石」。

examples.english: The museum has a rare collection of meteorites.
examples.chinese: 這家博物館收藏了罕見的隕石。

vocabularyNotes.title: granite
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，指「花崗岩」，一種質地非常堅硬的火成岩。故事中利用它與「granted」進行英文的諧音雙關。

examples.english: The kitchen countertops are made of solid granite.
examples.chinese: 廚房的流理台檯面是用純花崗岩製成的。

phraseNotes.title: taken for granite
phraseNotes.explanation: 這是作者刻意設計的雙關語（Pun）。英文常片語是「take something for granted」（把某事視為理所當然／不把某人的價值當一回事），這裡故意把 granted 換成發音相近的石頭種類 granite（花崗岩）。

examples.english: She felt unappreciated, like she was being taken for granted.
examples.chinese: 她覺得自己不被重視，就像被大家當作理所當然存在一樣。（正常片語用法）

sentences.englishOriginal: Except for poor Ricky, who quietly sat.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 除了可憐的里奇，他只能靜靜地坐在那裡。

phraseNotes.title: except for
phraseNotes.explanation: 介系詞片語，意思是「除了……之外」，用來指出整體中唯一的例外情況。

examples.english: The playground was empty except for a single dog.
examples.chinese: 除了有一隻狗之外，遊樂場上空無一人。

sentences.englishOriginal: You see, Rick couldn't roll because one side was flat.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 你瞧，里奇沒辦法滾動，是因為他有一面是扁平的。

vocabularyNotes.title: flat
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，意思是「平的、扁平的」，在故事中是里奇無法像其他球狀石頭一樣滾動的核心主因。

examples.english: The box provides a flat surface to write on.
examples.chinese: 這個盒子提供了一個可以寫字的平整表面。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: You see
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 口語中非常道地的插入語，意思是「你知道吧、你瞧、話說回來」，常用在向聽眾或對話夥伴解釋某個狀況或原因的開頭。

examples.english: You see, I’ve never done this type of work before.
examples.chinese: 你明白吧，我以前從來沒做過這種工作。

sentences.englishOriginal: But the rocks were determined, they were sure they could solve Ricky's flat-sided problem, and help him revolve.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 但是石頭們下定了決心，他們深信自己一定能解決里奇「有一面扁平」的難題，並幫助他旋轉滾動。

vocabularyNotes.title: determined
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 形容詞，意思是「意志堅定的、下定決心的」，後面常搭配不定詞 to + 動詞。

examples.english: She was determined to pass the driving test this time.
examples.chinese: 她下定決心這次一定要通過駕照考試。

vocabularyNotes.title: revolve
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「旋轉、轉動」，在這裡作為 roll 的高級同義詞，用來配合詩歌的押韻（solve/revolve）。

examples.english: The Earth takes one year to revolve around the sun.
examples.chinese: 地球繞太陽公轉一圈需要花一年的時間。

grammarNotes.title: Noun-adjective combination (flat-sided)
grammarNotes.explanation: 英文中常用「形容詞/名詞 + 名詞-ed」的形式來組成複合形容詞。這裡「flat-sided」字面意思是「扁平面的」，用來精準形容里奇的身體特徵。

examples.english: That sharp-eyed bird spotted the worm from far away.
examples.chinese: 那隻眼神銳利的鳥從很遠的地方就發現了那隻蟲子。

sentences.englishOriginal: So they pondered and thought, each straining his brain, 'til they looked up and saw it was starting to rain.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 於是他們沉思苦想，每個人都竭盡腦力，直到他們抬起頭，發現天空開始下起雨來。

vocabularyNotes.title: ponder
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，意思是「沉思、仔細考慮」，語氣比一般的 think 還要深刻、專注。

examples.english: He sat alone in the room, pondering his next move.
examples.chinese: 他獨自坐在房間裡，沉思著他的下一步棋。

phraseNotes.title: strain one's brain
phraseNotes.explanation: 生動的片語，字面意思是「拉緊、使勁使用大腦」，用來形容「絞盡腦汁、非常用力地思考」。

examples.english: I've been straining my brain all morning trying to remember her name.
examples.chinese: 我整個早上都在絞盡腦汁，試著想起她的名字。

sentences.englishOriginal: And that's when it hit that smart stone named Stu.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 就在那個時候，那顆名叫斯圖的聰明石頭突然靈光一閃。

grammarNotes.title: It hit someone that... (Sudden realization)
grammarNotes.explanation: 「It hits someone」是一個常見的語法結構，用來表達「某人突然意識到、某人突然靈光一閃或猛然想到某事」。故事中「it hit that smart stone」意思就是點子突然砸中了這顆石頭。

examples.english: It suddenly hit me that I had left my keys in the car.
examples.chinese: 我突然猛然想起我把鑰匙忘在車裡了。

nativeExpressionNotes.title: that's when...
nativeExpressionNotes.explanation: 用來強調某個特定時間點發生了關鍵事件，意思是「就在那個時候……」。在說故事時非常具有戲劇張力。

examples.english: We were about to leave, and that's when the phone rang.
examples.chinese: 我們正準備要離開，就在那個時候電話響了。

sentences.englishOriginal: He explained to them how they would get Rick to tumble.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 他向大家解釋了他們要如何讓里奇翻滾起來。

vocabularyNotes.title: tumble
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 動詞，指「翻滾、跌撞著滾動」，在這裡同樣用來生動形容石頭滾動的姿態。

examples.english: The golden leaves tumbled down from the old oak tree.
examples.chinese: 金黃色的葉子從那棵老橡樹上翻滾飄落下來。

phraseNotes.title: get someone to do something
phraseNotes.explanation: 使役動詞用法，意思是「說服、設法讓某人去完成某個動作」，強調經過一番努力或安排而達成的結果。

examples.english: I finally managed to get the engine to start.
examples.chinese: 我最後終於設法讓引擎發動了。

sentences.englishOriginal: She thought, as she watched her friends play on the hill, that there's always a way if there's also a will.
sentences.chineseTranslation: 當她看著朋友們在山丘上玩耍時，她心想，只要有決心，就一定會有辦法。

vocabularyNotes.title: will
vocabularyNotes.explanation: 名詞，在這裡不是助動詞「將要」，而是指「意志力、決心或意願」。

examples.english: Despite the structural difficulties, they had the will to complete the project.
examples.chinese: 儘管面臨結構性的困難，他們仍有完成這項計畫的決心。

grammarNotes.title: Proverb modification (Where there's a will, there's a way)
grammarNotes.explanation: 作者在這裡巧妙地改寫了經典英文諺語「Where there's a will, there's a way」（有志者事竟成）。將其拆開並重新融入詩句中，呼應了故事中朋友們堅持不懈的精神。

examples.english: Don't give up on your dreams; remember that where there's a will, there's a way.
examples.chinese: 別放棄你的夢想；記住，有志者事竟成。
